samples.question: What waiting period applies to pre-existing diseases?
samples.query_used: What waiting period applies to pre-existing diseases?
samples.answer_preview:
36 months

References:
- [1] HealthInsurance_Policy.pdf - Page 40
- [2] HealthInsurance_Policy.pdf - Page 70
- [3] HealthInsurance_Policy.pdf - Page 70
- [4] HealthInsurance_Policy.pdf - Page 10
samples.note: Pre-existing diseases are excluded until the expiry of 36 months of continuous coverage [Source 2]. Source 1 notes this 36-month period can be modified only if the optional PED wait period modification is chosen [Source 1].

samples.question: How can an insured person avail cashless treatment at a network provider?
samples.query_used: How can an insured person avail cashless treatment at a network provider?
samples.answer_preview:
Submit a duly filled pre-authorization form through the network provider, and produce the Health card plus a valid photo ID; cashless treatment is available only after the Company/Assistance Service Provider approves it in writing [Source 1][Source 4].

References:
- [1] HealthInsurance_Policy.pdf - Page 90
- [2] HealthInsurance_Policy.pdf - Page 91
- [3] HealthInsurance_Policy.pdf - Page 91
- [4] HealthInsurance_Policy.pdf - Page 90
samples.note: The evidence states the insured person must preserve and produce the Health card at a Network Provider, submit a pre-authorization form via the Network Provider, provide identification documents, and wait for written approval before cashless facility can be availed [Source 1][Source 4].

samples.question: What is the main focus of the pancreas8 paper?
samples.query_used: Summarize the main objective of the Pancreas8 study/paper—what problem does it primarily address?
samples.note: The provided excerpts discuss biomarkers, genomics/proteomics, and multimodal/TME monitoring, but they do not state the main focus of the pancreas8 paper directly [Source 1].

samples.question: What was the baseline bias AUC and what did extreme standardization reduce it to?
samples.query_used: What was the baseline bias AUC and what did extreme standardization reduce it to?
samples.answer_preview:
Baseline bias AUC was 0.866, and extreme standardization reduced it to 0.569 [Source 1].

References:
- [1] Final_Thesis_Leander_Antony_A.pdf - Page 89
- [2] Final_Thesis_Leander_Antony_A.pdf - Page 74
- [3] Final_Thesis_Leander_Antony_A.pdf - Page 56
- [4] Final_Thesis_Leander_Antony_A.pdf - Page 56
samples.note: The evidence explicitly states the baseline pixel-mean bias AUC was 0.866 and that extreme standardization reduced the bias AUC to 0.569 [Source 1].
